purpose.summary: This COBOL program contains subroutines related to inserting child segments into a GSAM database and handling abnormal termination. It includes logic to call IMS DL/I functions and check PCB status after the call.

inputs.name: PADFLPCB
inputs.description: IMS PCB (Program Communication Block) used for GSAM database access.

inputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS
inputs.description: Data segment to be inserted into the GSAM database.

called_programs.purpose: To perform IMS DL/I database operations, specifically inserting a segment.

business_rules.description: If the PCB status after the GSAM insert call is not spaces, the program abends.
business_rules.logic_summary: Checks PADFL-PCB-STATUS for non-space value. If not spaces, the program displays error messages and abends.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph inserts a child segment into a GSAM database. It calls the CBLTDLI routine with the ISRT function code, the PADFLPCB, and the PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS segment. After the call, it checks the PADFL-PCB-STATUS field in the PADFLPCB. If the status is not spaces, indicating an error, it displays diagnostic information and calls the 9999-ABEND paragraph to terminate the program. The purpose of this paragraph is to add a new segment to the GSAM database and ensure the operation was successful by checking the PCB status. It consumes the PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS segment as input and uses the PADFLPCB to communicate with the IMS database. It calls CBLTDLI to perform the database operation and 9999-ABEND for error handling. The business logic involves validating the PCB status after the database call.

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 3200-INSERT-CHILD-SEG-GSAM paragraph. It contains a simple EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph. It does not perform any specific data manipulation, error handling, or call any other paragraphs. Its sole purpose is to provide a standard exit point, ensuring consistent program flow. It consumes no input and produces no output. The business logic is simply to return control to the caller. The paragraph is called by 3200-INSERT-CHILD-SEG-GSAM after the IMS call and error checking.

This paragraph is responsible for closing files used by the program. Currently, it only contains a DISPLAY statement indicating that the file is being closed. It's likely a placeholder or part of incomplete functionality. It does not take any input or produce any specific output other than the display message. It does not perform any error handling or call any other paragraphs. The business logic is limited to displaying a message. The paragraph's purpose is to prepare for program termination by closing files, although the specific file to be closed is not evident from the code.

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 4000-FILE-CLOSE paragraph. It contains a simple EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph. It does not perform any specific data manipulation, error handling, or call any other paragraphs. Its sole purpose is to provide a standard exit point, ensuring consistent program flow. It consumes no input and produces no output. The business logic is simply to return control to the caller. The paragraph is likely called by 4000-FILE-CLOSE after displaying the closing message.

This paragraph handles abnormal program termination. It displays a message indicating that the program is abending, sets the RETURN-CODE to 16, and then executes a GOBACK statement to terminate the program. It does not take any input other than the implicit program state. It sets the RETURN-CODE as output, which can be used by the calling job or system to determine the reason for the abend. It does not perform any specific error handling other than setting the return code. It does not call any other paragraphs. The business logic is to signal an unrecoverable error and terminate the program. This paragraph is called by 3200-INSERT-CHILD-SEG-GSAM when the PCB status indicates an error after the GSAM insert call.

This paragraph serves as a standard exit point. It contains an EXIT statement, returning control to the calling routine. It performs no data manipulation, error handling, or calls to other paragraphs. It consumes no input and produces no output. The business logic is simply to return control to the caller. This paragraph likely serves as a placeholder for a more comprehensive exit routine, or is used in other parts of the program not shown in this snippet.

error_handling.action: DISPLAY error messages and ABEND with RETURN-CODE 16
